activation_provenance.honesty: generalized-block APPROXIMATION: block-N attention is a from-config forward (RoPE theta 150000, GQA 64/8, sinks, sliding-window inactive at seq<128), exactly as the block-0 forward is; it is valid ONLY for the RELATIVE orig-vs-packed divergence, where the shared residual + shared reference SwiGLU largely cancel the approximation. Not real Harmony text, no holdout corpus; authorizes no Escape Receipt / Event Horizon seal.
activation_provenance.token_source: synthetic Harmony-ish id sequences (SAME tokens at every layer) pushed through the REAL embedding + block-N attention + block-N mlp-norm path

base_execution_provider.name: m2_shared_lookup_linear
base_execution_provider.note: Gen-M lookup-linear is EXACT parity to Gen-F direct-compact; it lowers logical FLOPs / movement / dispatches but does NOT change the measured divergence. Gen-F direct-compact is the wall-safe default provider.

rows.cpu_impl: gravity_frontier_g3 numpy reference forward (generalized block N)
rows.metal_impl: gravity_forge MPS (_kmeans/_assign) for the packer fit
rows.note: PQ + residual-energy protected islands (heavy-tail reserve)
rows.stopping_rule: per (tensor_class, layer) promote the representation with the HIGHEST layer_hidden_state_cosine within the exact per-matrix budget; source_native controls are reference boundaries and are excluded; then record whether the layer-0 winner still wins at the mid (18) and late (35) layers (cross-layer transfer)

scientific_question: Does the full-rank sub-bit representation that best preserves a COMPLETE GPT-OSS layer 0 still best preserve the residual stream at a middle (18) and a late (35) layer? i.e. does the winning geometry TRANSFER across depth?

search_doctrine.class_isolation: each candidate replaces ONLY its tensor class; the router stays native
search_doctrine.execution_note: execution_generation=M is quality-NEUTRAL (m2_shared_lookup_linear is EXACT parity to Gen-F direct-compact); it changes mechanical cost, never the measured divergence
search_doctrine.transfer_verdict: compare the per-layer winner family to the layer-0 winner family per tensor_class; report transfers_to_mid / transfers_to_late
